pattern_recognition: Tree Dynamic Programming; this problem involves finding maximum sums in a tree structure, which often indicates the use of recursion and dynamic programming techniques.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Calculate the path sum for every possible path in the tree. This involves traversing the tree multiple times to find all paths.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Generate all paths from each node to every other node. 2. Calculate the sum for each path. 3. Keep track of the maximum sum encountered.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Use a depth-first search (DFS) to calculate the maximum path sum while keeping track of the maximum sum found during the traversal.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Perform a DFS traversal of the tree. 2. For each node, calculate the maximum path sum that can be obtained from that node. 3. Update the global maximum path sum whenever a new maximum is found.

dry_run: Consider the binary tree: [1, 2, 3]. The maximum path sum can be found by starting at node 2, going to node 1, and then to node 3. The path is 2 -> 1 -> 3, which sums to 6. The algorithm will traverse the tree, calculating sums for paths like 2 -> 1 and 1 -> 3, and ultimately find that the maximum path sum is 6.
edge_cases: An empty tree should return 0. A tree with a single node should return the value of that node. Trees with duplicate values should be handled correctly, ensuring that the maximum path sum is calculated accurately. Large input sizes should be tested to ensure efficiency, and potential overflow should be considered when summing large values.
common_pitfalls: 1. Failing to account for negative values in the path sum can lead to incorrect results. 2. Not updating the global maximum correctly during the traversal can cause the algorithm to miss the true maximum path sum. 3. Inefficient path generation in the brute force approach can lead to excessive computation time, especially for larger trees.
